title: MessageBrokerContract
description: Standard message envelope used by all microservices when publishing or consuming events through RabbitMQ or Kafka. Every message MUST conform to this schema to be accepted by consumers.

specVersion.description: CloudEvents specification version this message adheres to.

id.description: Universally unique identifier for this specific message instance. Generated by the producer. Used for idempotency checks by consumers.

source.description: URI-reference identifying the context in which the event occurred. Follows the pattern: /service-name/aggregate-type.

type.description: Reverse-DNS event type identifier. Must match a known event defined in EventTypes.json.

dataContentType.description: MIME type of the data payload.

dataSchema.description: URI pointing to the JSON Schema describing the data payload. Optional but recommended.

subject.description: The entity subject of this event. Typically the aggregate ID.

time.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (UTC) at which the event occurred.

tenantId.description: Identifier of the SaaS tenant that owns this event. Required for all tenant-scoped events.

correlationId.description: Correlation ID propagated across the entire request chain for distributed tracing.

causationId.description: ID of the message that caused this message to be produced. Used to build causality chains.

sagaId.description: Identifier of the Saga orchestration instance this message belongs to, if applicable.

sagaStep.description: Named step within the saga workflow.

version.description: Schema version of the data payload. Consumers should use this for backward-compatible deserialization.

retryCount.description: Number of times this message has been redelivered. Used by consumers for poison-message detection.

priority.description: Message priority (0 = lowest, 9 = highest). Supported by RabbitMQ priority queues.

expiresAt.description: Optional TTL for the event. Consumers MUST discard messages received after this time.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata attached by the producer. Not processed by the broker contract layer.

data.description: The event payload. Structure is defined by the specific event schema referenced in dataSchema.
